relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Joseph came to sick Jacob with Manasseh and Ephraim.
Israel strengthened himself to bless.
He remembered El Shaddai at Luz.
He remembered fruitfulness, peoplehood, and land.
Joseph’s sons were adopted into Israel’s inheritance.
Later children would stand under their brothers’ names.
Rachel’s death on the road remained alive in Jacob’s memory.
Israel saw Joseph’s sons and asked who they were.
Joseph named them as divine gifts, and Jacob called them near for blessing.
Dim eyes gave way to kisses and embrace.
Jacob had not expected Joseph’s face, yet now saw his seed.
Joseph bowed before the blessing moment.
Joseph arranged the sons according to birth order.
Israel crossed his hands and reversed the order.
He blessed through the God who had shepherded his whole life.
The redeeming messenger was invoked over the boys.
Joseph tried to correct the crossed blessing.
He pointed to Manasseh as firstborn.
Jacob knew, yet chose the younger for greater fullness.
Ephraim was set before Manasseh in Israel’s blessing.
Jacob spoke death, divine presence, and return.
Joseph received an extra portion above his brothers.
